name: Razorpay Payment Validation — Mutation Testing
scenario: Razorpay's payment validation has 95% line coverage but a production bug slipped through. Investigation reveals the tests never verify boundary conditions or side-effect calls. Achieve 85%+ mutation score with PITest.

core.description: PaymentValidator @Service: validate(merchantId, amount, gateway). Amount: 1-1_000_000. Gateway: Set of HDFC, VISA, RUPAY, AXIS. MerchantId: not blank. Throws IllegalArgumentException for each failure.
AuditLogger interface + verify calls: audit.log(event, merchantId) called on successful validation. NOT called on validation failure.
Boundary tests for amount: 0 → throws, 1 → valid, 1_000_000 → valid, 1_000_001 → throws. These 4 tests kill ConditionalBoundary mutations.
Branch tests: null merchantId, blank merchantId, invalid gateway — each independently throws with the correct exception message.
Mockito verify assertions: verify(audit).log("validated", merchantId) on success. verifyNoInteractions(audit) on any failure.
PITest pom.xml config: targetClasses, targetTests, mutationThreshold=80, pitest-junit5-plugin dependency. Maven profile 'pitest-ci'.
@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension) tests: minimum 9 tests, all pass. Mutation score must be 85%+ when PITest is run.
README: explains mutation score vs line coverage, with one example of a surviving mutation and the test that kills it.

bonus.description: PaymentRouter with 4-branch routing logic. Separate mutation test class killing ConditionalBoundary mutations at each branch boundary. 8 tests.
Equivalent mutant documentation: identify 2 surviving mutations that are equivalent (e.g., log.debug removal) and document why they're acceptable in a MutationAnalysis.md file.
OrderEligibility (4-condition AND): eligible(age, kyc, balance, riskScore). Write 11 tests achieving near-100% mutation score. All boundary values covered.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. ConditionalBoundary kills: test exactly AT boundary (e.g., amount=1 to kill mutation of < 1 to <= 1), and ONE value on each side. VoidMethodCall kills: use Mockito verify(mock).method(exactArgs) — not just verify(mock).method(any()). Exact argument matching is stronger and kills more mutations. Equivalent mutants: log.debug() removal is typically equivalent — debug logging has no functional effect. mutationThreshold: build fails if score below threshold — 80 means 80% of non-equivalent mutations must be killed by tests. PITest skips: use -DskipMutationTests=true for local builds; enable in CI via Maven profile.
